html:
The Night Armor is a wearable Magic Armor, often paired up with a Dark Sword. It requires 5 Nightmare Fuel and 3 Papyrus to craft and a Shadow Manipulator to prototype. It is one of Maxwell's starting items in the single player game.

It has 750 durability and absorbs 95% of physical damage. The Night Armor drains Sanity by 10/min while worn and decreases Sanity by 10% of total damage taken (not only absorbed by the armor).

Example: If a player wearing Night Armor is hit by a Spider Queen (80 ), then

  • 80 × 0.95 = 76 damage is taken by Night Armor.
  • 80 − 76 = 4 damage is taken by the player's .
  • The player's will be decreased by 80 × 0.1 = 8.

Downloadable Content

In the Hamlet DLC, Night Armors can be bought for 20 Oincs at The Flying Pig Arcane Shop in the Pig City. Night Armors can be used to craft a Vortex Cloak.

Usage

×5

Tips

  • It is useful to also equip a head-slot item which can boost sanity, such as the Tam o' Shanter, helping to offset the sanity drain while still having very high physical protection.
  • The Night Armor is especially useful on Wanda in her old form, nullifying the sanity drain, and allowing her to benefit from the bonus damage on shadow weapons, while staying more safe.

Trivia

  • The Night Armor was added in the Insanity! update.
  • When paired with the Shelmet or the Thulecite Crown, the player is able to absorb 99.5% of incoming damage (doesn't apply to ), enough to survive a Gunpowder explosion, losing 1 and 20 .
  • Wickerbottom states the armor transfers the damage into another dimension.
  • The Night Armor makes a "warping" noise when the player moves while wearing it.

Gallery

  • Wilson wearing Night Armor.
  • Willow wearing Night Armor.
  • Wolfgang wearing Night Armor.
  • Wendy wearing Night Armor.
  • WX-78 wearing Night Armor.
  • Wickerbottom wearing Night Armor.
  • Woodie wearing Night Armor.
  • Wes wearing Night Armor.
  • Maxwell wearing Night Armor.
  • Wigfrid wearing Night Armor.
  • Webber wearing Night Armor.
  • Walani wearing Night Armor.
  • Wilbur wearing Night Armor.
  • Warly wearing Night Armor.
  • Woodlegs wearing Night Armor.
  • Wilba wearing Night Armor.
  • Wormwood wearing Night Armor.
  • Wheeler wearing Night Armor.
  • Winona wearing Night Armor.
  • Wortox wearing Night Armor.
  • Wurt wearing Night Armor.
  • Original HD icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Night Armor as seen in the poster for the Insanity! update.

"armor_sanity"
“Wearing this makes me feel safe and insecure.”
–Wilson
“Like being wrapped in smoke.”
–Willow
“Is like wearing scary little rag!”
–Wolfgang
“A perfectly safe way to go insane.”
–Wendy
“I WILL PROTECT MYSELF WITH NOTHINGNESS”
–WX-78
“A protective shroud that transfers attacks to another dimension.”
–Wickerbottom
“It makes me queasy to look at.”
–Woodie
“Time to wrap myself in nightmares.”
–Maxwell
“Armor made from the dark fuel! I cannot wait to see its effects.”
–Wagstaff
“Ströng, but I find my mind wanders...”
–Wigfrid
“I'm a bit uneasy wearing this, but it's effective.”
–Webber
“Eyuk! It makes my skin crawl.”
–Walani
“Am I crazy to wear this?”
–Warly
“Feels good ta put it on.”
–Woodlegs
“'TIS ARMOR MADE OF NIGHTMARE BITS”
–Wilba
“Brain Guard”
–Wormwood
“Looks... safe?”
–Wheeler
“Soothingly unsettling.”
–Winona
“Keeps the flesh body safe! The mind's another story.”
–Wortox
“Don't like it, flurp!!”
–Wurt
“Wait... is this dangerous?”
–Walter
“Shadow magic is just a tool after all, I can use it to my advantage.”
–Wanda
The Night Armor is a wearable Magic Armor, often paired up with a Dark Sword. It requires 5 Nightmare Fuel and 3 Papyrus to craft and a Shadow Manipulator to prototype. It is one of Maxwell's starting items in the single player game.
It has 750 durability and absorbs 95% of physical damage. The Night Armor drains Sanity by 10/min while worn and decreases Sanity by 10% of total damage taken (not only absorbed by the armor).
Example: If a player wearing Night Armor is hit by a Spider Queen (80 ), then
80 × 0.95 = 76 damage is taken by Night Armor.
80 − 76 = 4 damage is taken by the player's .
The player's will be decreased by 80 × 0.1 = 8.
Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
In the Hamlet DLC, Night Armors can be bought for 20 Oincs at The Flying Pig Arcane Shop in the Pig City. Night Armors can be used to craft a Vortex Cloak.
Usage[edit | edit source]
×5
Tips[edit | edit source]
It is useful to also equip a head-slot item which can boost sanity, such as the Tam o' Shanter, helping to offset the sanity drain while still having very high physical protection.
The Night Armor is especially useful on Wanda in her old form, nullifying the sanity drain, and allowing her to benefit from the bonus damage on shadow weapons, while staying more safe.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Night Armor was added in the Insanity! update.
When paired with the Shelmet or the Thulecite Crown, the player is able to absorb 99.5% of incoming damage (doesn't apply to ), enough to survive a Gunpowder explosion, losing 1 and 20 .
Wickerbottom states the armor transfers the damage into another dimension.
The Night Armor makes a "warping" noise when the player moves while wearing it.

It requires 5 [[Nightmare Fuel]] and 3 [[Papyrus]] to craft and a [[Shadow Manipulator]] to prototype. It is one of [[Maxwell (character)|Maxwell]]'s starting items in the single player game. It has 750 durability and absorbs 95% of physical damage. The Night Armor drains [[Sanity]] by 10/min while worn and decreases Sanity by 10% of total damage taken (not only absorbed by the armor). Example: If a player wearing Night Armor is hit by a [[Spider Queen]] (80 {{Hp}}), then *80 × 0.95 = 76 damage is taken by Night Armor. *80 − 76 = 4 damage is taken by the player's {{hp}}. *The player's {{sanity}} will be decreased by 80 × 0.1 = 8. == {{Pic32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content == In the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC, Night Armors can be bought for 20 [[Oinc]]s at [[The Flying Pig Arcane Shop]] in the [[Pig City]]. Night Armors can be used to craft a [[Vortex Cloak]]. == {{Pic32|Icon Tools}} Usage == {{Recipe |item1 = Night Armor |count1 = 1 |item2 = Dark Tatters |count2 = 5 |tool = Blueprint |result = Vortex Cloak |dlc= Hamlet}} == {{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips == * It is useful to also equip a head-slot item which can boost [[sanity]], such as the [[Tam o' Shanter]], helping to offset the sanity drain while still having very high physical protection. *The Night Armor is especially useful on [[Wanda]] in her old form, nullifying the sanity drain, and allowing her to benefit from the bonus damage on shadow weapons, while staying more safe. == {{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * The Night Armor was added in the ''[[Don't Starve/Version History#Insanity!|Insanity!]]'' update. * When paired with the [[Shelmet]] or the [[Thulecite Crown]], the player is able to absorb 99.5% of incoming damage (doesn't apply to {{Pic24|Don't Starve Together icon}}), enough to survive a [[Gunpowder]] explosion, losing 1 {{Hp}} and 20 {{Sanity}}. * [[Wickerbottom]] states the armor transfers the damage into another dimension. * The Night Armor makes a "warping" noise when the player moves while wearing it. == {{Pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery == Night Armor Wilson.png|[[Wilson]] wearing Night Armor.
